---
title: "within_condition_binomial_codon"
subtitle: "ribostall stall_sites — enrichment stats"
date: 2026-06-02
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    embed-resources: true
    fig-cap-location: bottom
    include-in-header:
      text: |
        <style>
        table td, table th { padding-top: 0.5em; padding-bottom: 0.5em; }
        </style>
  pdf:
    toc: true
    fig-cap-location: bottom
    include-in-header:
      text: |
        \usepackage{needspace}
        \renewcommand{\arraystretch}{1.5}
tbl-colwidths: [8, 5, 5, 17, 14, 14, 37]
execute:
  echo: false
---

## Composite figure

![BWM 3x3 grid of unweighted codon binomial volcano panels: rows are the three BWM timepoints (top to bottom: Day 0, Day 5, Day 10); columns are ribosome sites in A / P / E order. Each panel plots -log10(FDR) (y) against Log2 Enrichment (x, range ~-10 to +10). Triangles mark FDR<0.05 hits; circles are non-significant. Each glyph is one of 61 sense codons labelled by its three-letter code and coloured by the amino-acid property of its translation (red = acidic, blue = basic, green = hydrophobic, orange = neutral, purple = polar). Horizontal dashed line is FDR = 0.05; vertical dashed lines are at log2 = +/-0.5.](../../plots/within_condition/codon/composite/unweighted/BWM_volcano_grid.png){fig-pos="H" width=92%}

![control 3x3 grid of unweighted codon binomial volcano panels: rows are the three control timepoints (top to bottom: Day 0, Day 5, Day 10); columns are ribosome sites in A / P / E order. Axes, glyph labelling, colour key, and dashed reference lines are identical to the BWM grid above.](../../plots/within_condition/codon/composite/unweighted/control_volcano_grid.png){fig-pos="H" width=92%}

Each glyph is one sense codon tested against its per-group, per-site background frequency (`bg_freq`); a panel is one (group, site) BH-FDR family of 61 codons. Positive Log2 Enrichment means the codon is over-represented at stall sites relative to its background frequency in that group's transcriptome window; negative means under-represented. The test is **within-group**: BWM and control are not compared directly here — that contrast lives in the per-timepoint Fisher sister file. Panels are arranged in A / P / E ribosome-site order across columns.

---

## Overview

- **CSV:** `within_condition_binomial_codon.csv`
- **Family (from `_INDEX.md`):** `within_condition_binomial`
- **Sister CSV(s):**
  - `within_condition_binomial_aa.csv`
- **Test:** One-sample binomial test (k=`stall_count` out of n=`total_n` vs H0: p=`bg_freq`, two-sided); BH-FDR within each (group, site) family of 61 sense codons
- **Design:** Per (group, site, codon) one-sample binomial: observed `stall_count` is tested against the binomial null `Binomial(total_n, bg_freq)` where `bg_freq` is the codon frequency in that group's transcriptome window. 6 groups (BWM and control x d0/d5/d10) x 3 sites (A/P/E) x 61 sense codons = 1098 tests; replicates are pooled before the test (pseudoreplicated); BH-FDR is applied independently within each of 18 (group, site) families of 61 codons. The test is **within-group**: each group is tested against its own background independently and never against another group.

### Family-level caveats

- **pseudorep** — 2 replicates within a group are summed before the binomial test; per-replicate variation is invisible to the statistic, so p-values are anti-conservative.
- **iid-violation-binomial** — the binomial test assumes the `total_n` counted positions are *i.i.d.*, short for **independent and identically distributed**. In plain terms it treats every counted position like a separate, independent coin-flip, each with the same probability (`bg_freq`) of landing on this codon. Stall sites break both halves of that assumption. They are not *independent*: one stall on a transcript pulls in neighbouring positions on that same transcript, so many of the counts rise and fall together instead of standing on their own. They are not *identically distributed*: transcripts differ in coverage and sequence, so the per-position probability is not really constant. Because the test believes it has far more independent evidence than it actually does, it reports p-values far smaller (more "certain") than the data warrant. Headline-grade tiny p-values (down to 1e-97 in this file) reflect this broken assumption more than per-feature biological signal — read `log2_enrichment` and `stall_count`, not p magnitude.
- **bg-pseudocount-1e-6** — when a codon is missing from the per-group background, `bg_freq` is floored at 1e-6 (upstream pipeline behaviour); for genuinely absent codons this produces extreme `log2_enrichment` when k>0. Mostly a rare-codon corner case (does not affect any Top-hits row here, where all `bg_freq` > 0.001).
- **bh-per-(group,site)** — BH-FDR is per (group, site) family of 61 codons. `p_adj` means "corrected within this group at this site," not across the whole CSV.

---

## Headline

Within-group binomial against `bg_freq`, 6 groups (BWM and control x d0/d5/d10) x 3 sites (A/P/E) x 61 sense codons = 1098 tests; BH-FDR per (group, site) family of 61. 491/1098 hits at FDR<0.05 (44.7%; 552/1098 at FDR<0.10). File min `p_adj` = 3.53e-97 at control_day_0 P:GAT (`log2_enrichment` = +0.828, `stall_count` = 1558, `total_n` = 27732); this same cell sits in the cross-group concordance set with mean `log2_enrichment` = +0.98 across the 6 groups. The dominant structure of this file is **cross-group concordance**: the largest-magnitude cells move in the same direction across all 6 groups. Of the 183 (codon, site) cells, 95 share a single sign across all six groups (50 enriched + 45 depleted) and 88 show at least one cross-group sign disagreement; the discordant cells sit at low magnitude or rest on rare-codon counts (see the three cross-group sub-tables below). The 10 largest-magnitude shared-direction cells (by mean `log2_enrichment` across the 6 groups) are: P:TCC (Ser) -1.04, P:GCC (Ala) -1.02, P:GAT (Asp) +0.98, E:GCC (Ala) -0.94, A:GCC (Ala) -0.90, P:AAT (Asn) +0.88, E:GCT (Ala) -0.86, A:GCT (Ala) -0.82, E:AAA (Lys) +0.78, P:GGT (Gly) +0.74. The broad lower-tail span of `p_adj` from 3.53e-97 to ~1.0 is shape-driven by `iid-violation-binomial` amplification at high `stall_count` and is not a faithful priority signal across cells — magnitude + cross-group reproducibility is the anchor.

## Top hits

The effect column is `log2_enrichment` = log2(observed `stall_freq` / `bg_freq`); positive means the codon is over-represented at stall sites in that group, negative means under-represented. The three **cross-group** tables below (concordant enrichment, concordant depletion, and discordant cells across the 6 groups) are the file's strongest reproducibility readout and the headline sub-tabulation. Each table shows every cell that reached FDR<0.05 in at least 2 of the 6 groups — a reproducibility floor on the `#sig` axis (groups with FDR<0.05, of 6), not a fixed row cap — then ranks the kept cells by `#sig` then `min count`, never by p magnitude: the file's `p-magnitude-anchored-ranking` caveat is the reason: `iid-violation-binomial` and common-codon `bg_freq` tightness inflate p magnitudes for cells with modest effect at large n. The `aa` column gives the single-letter amino-acid translation of each codon (no parenthetical expansion); the codon cell itself stays as the bare three-letter codon.

```{=latex}
\needspace{22\baselineskip}
```

Each (site, codon) cell pools the six per-group `log2_enrichment` values (the binomial effect vs that group's `bg_freq`). In the `log2_enrichment` column the six values are listed on two lines — **BWM** day_0, day_5, day_10 on the first line, then **control** day_0, day_5, day_10 on the second. **Concordant** cells share one sign across all 6 groups (enrichment = all positive, depletion = all negative); **discordant** cells have at least one sign disagreement across the 6 groups. `#sig` counts the groups with `p_adj` < 0.05 (out of 6); `min count` is the smallest `observed_count` across the 6 groups (the weakest group's data support). Each table shows every cell with `#sig` >= 2 (significant in at least 2 of the 6 groups), displayed sorted by site (A / P / E), then `#sig` desc, then `min count` desc. In the data there are 50 concordant-enriched, 45 concordant-depleted, and 88 discordant cells; after the `#sig` >= 2 floor the tables show 44, 37, and 25 cells respectively (cells significant in fewer than 2 groups are sign-concordant or sign-discordant by coincidence and are omitted). The `aa` column is the single-letter amino-acid translation of each codon (no parenthetical expansion); the codon cell stays bare.

### Concordant enrichment — significant in >= 2 of 6 groups

| Site | codon | aa | log2_enrichment | min count | #sig | flag |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| A | GAG | E | +0.38, +0.31, +0.58 <br>`\newline`{=latex} +0.31, +0.45, +0.43 | 381 | 6/6 | iid-amp |

```{=latex}
\clearpage
```

| E | TCT | S | -0.73, -0.16, -0.28 <br>`\newline`{=latex} -0.52, -0.35, -0.56 | 45 | 4/6 | rare-codon (BWM d0, BWM d10, ctrl d10) |

: Concordant depletion — all 6 groups depleted; cells significant in >= 2 of 6 groups, ranked by #sig then min count. {tbl-colwidths="[6,7,4,40,8,7,28]"}

```{=latex}
\needspace{22\baselineskip}
```

### Discordant — significant in >= 2 of 6 groups

| Site | codon | aa | log2_enrichment | min count | #sig | flag |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| A | GGA | G | +0.18, +0.07, +0.45 <br>`\newline`{=latex} -0.14, +0.13, +0.36 | 455 | 5/6 | iid-amp, bg-tight |
| A | ATC | I | -0.22, -0.01, -0.01 <br>`\newline`{=latex} -0.12, +0.21, +0.06 | 231 | 3/6 |  |
| A | TGC | C | +0.17, -0.44, -0.47 <br>`\newline`{=latex} -0.12, -0.51, +0.20 | 53 | 3/6 | rare-codon (all but ctrl d0) |
| A | AAT | N | -0.08, +0.41, +0.37 <br>`\newline`{=latex} +0.22, +0.51, +0.25 | 34 | 3/6 | rare-codon (BWM d0, BWM d10, ctrl d10) |
| A | AGC | S | +0.05, -0.72, -0.60 <br>`\newline`{=latex} -0.47, -0.43, -0.46 | 29 | 3/6 | rare-codon (all but ctrl d0) |
| A | TTT | F | -0.18, +0.49, +0.60 <br>`\newline`{=latex} +0.34, +0.46, +0.28 | 16 | 3/6 | rare-codon (all but ctrl d0) |
| A | CCA | P | +0.08, -0.07, -0.13 <br>`\newline`{=latex} -0.12, -0.22, +0.10 | 227 | 2/6 |  |
| A | CAA | Q | +0.08, +0.02, -0.36 <br>`\newline`{=latex} -0.02, -0.08, -0.59 | 126 | 2/6 |  |
| A | CGC | R | -0.23, -0.09, -0.05 <br>`\newline`{=latex} -0.20, -0.25, +0.24 | 104 | 2/6 |  |
| A | TGG | W | +0.77, +0.02, +0.33 <br>`\newline`{=latex} +0.60, -0.04, +0.16 | 78 | 2/6 | iid-amp, rare-codon (BWM d0, BWM d10, ctrl d5, ctrl d10) |
| A | GGT | G | +0.20, -0.02, +0.13 <br>`\newline`{=latex} -0.41, -0.16, -0.54 | 46 | 2/6 | rare-codon (all but ctrl d0) |
| A | TCG | S | +0.69, +0.12, +0.19 <br>`\newline`{=latex} +0.20, -0.03, +0.07 | 44 | 2/6 | rare-codon (all but ctrl d0) |
| A | GTG | V | +0.61, +0.16, -0.23 <br>`\newline`{=latex} +0.46, +0.28, +0.22 | 35 | 2/6 | rare-codon (all but ctrl d0) |
| P | AAG | K | +0.14, -0.03, -0.23 <br>`\newline`{=latex} +0.04, -0.23, -0.37 | 482 | 4/6 | bg-tight |
| P | GGA | G | +0.19, +0.03, +0.39 <br>`\newline`{=latex} -0.09, +0.07, +0.38 | 458 | 4/6 | iid-amp, bg-tight |
| P | AGA | R | +0.44, +0.29, -0.02 <br>`\newline`{=latex} +0.26, +0.13, +0.12 | 128 | 3/6 |  |
| P | CTG | L | -0.20, -0.46, +0.03 <br>`\newline`{=latex} -0.46, -0.84, -0.71 | 16 | 3/6 | rare-codon (all but ctrl d0) |
| P | TGT | C | -0.36, +0.54, +0.55 <br>`\newline`{=latex} +0.39, +0.45, +0.83 | 11 | 3/6 | rare-codon (all but ctrl d0) |
| P | ATC | I | +0.02, +0.02, -0.08 <br>`\newline`{=latex} -0.01, +0.16, -0.19 | 273 | 2/6 |  |
| P | ATG | M | +0.23, -0.05, -0.08 <br>`\newline`{=latex} +0.14, -0.17, -0.38 | 114 | 2/6 |  |
| P | GCA | A | +0.09, -0.62, -0.19 <br>`\newline`{=latex} -0.24, -0.41, -0.25 | 28 | 2/6 | rare-codon (all but ctrl d0) |
| E | GTT | V | -0.04, -0.20, -0.27 <br>`\newline`{=latex} +0.03, -0.27, -0.42 | 148 | 3/6 |  |
| E | GCA | A | -0.40, -0.44, +0.05 <br>`\newline`{=latex} -0.36, -0.57, -0.56 | 22 | 3/6 | rare-codon (all but ctrl d0) |
| E | GGA | G | -0.09, -0.17, +0.13 <br>`\newline`{=latex} -0.24, +0.03, -0.01 | 377 | 2/6 | iid-amp, bg-tight |
| E | ATC | I | +0.05, +0.10, -0.28 <br>`\newline`{=latex} +0.14, -0.11, +0.11 | 257 | 2/6 |  |

: Discordant — at least one sign disagreement across the 6 groups; cells significant in >= 2 of 6 groups, ranked by #sig then min count. {tbl-colwidths="[6,7,4,40,8,7,28]"}

### Flag glossary

- `iid-amp` — short for "**i.i.d.-violation amplified**" (i.i.d. = independent and identically distributed; see the `iid-violation-binomial` caveat under Family-level caveats for the full plain-language explanation). Applied to every Top-hits row with `p_adj` < 1e-10. The short version: the binomial test assumes every counted position is an independent coin-flip, but stall positions on the same transcript move together — so the test acts as if it has far more independent evidence than it does, and even a modest enrichment gets an astronomically tiny p. The flag is a reminder that this row's p looks tiny because of the broken independence assumption, not because the effect is biologically large. Read it by `log2_enrichment` + reproducibility, never by how small the p is. 110 of 1098 rows (10.0%) carry `iid-amp`.
- `bg-tight` — short for "**background-frequency tight**," meaning a narrow null. Applied to rows for a common codon (`bg_freq` > 0.05; AAG and GGA are the cells in Top hits that cross this threshold). For a common codon counted over tens of thousands of positions, the range of counts the binomial expects purely by chance (its null distribution) is very narrow, so even a small departure from `bg_freq` lands far out in the tail and produces a tiny p. Same upshot as `iid-amp`: the small p reflects how sharp the null is at large n, not a large biological effect. The two flags co-occur on AAG-at-E rows across all 6 groups.
- `rare-codon` — at least one of the 6 groups has `observed_count` below the stability threshold (k<100). The flag names the low-count groups — it lists them (e.g. `rare-codon (BWM d0, BWM d10)`), collapses to `(all but <group>)` when only one group clears the threshold, and to `(all)` when none do. Direction is reliable; magnitude is not. TGG (Trp, single-codon) is the canonical rare-codon case that cannot be rescued by synonym aggregation; a genuinely rare codon such as ATA (Ile) carries `rare-codon (all)`.

## Numbers at a glance

- `n_tests`: 1098 (6 groups x 3 sites x 61 sense codons)
- `n_significant` (adjusted-p < 0.05): 491 (44.7%)
- `n_significant` (adjusted-p < 0.10): 552 (50.3%)
- `min adjusted-p`: 3.53e-97 (control_day_0, P site, GAT; `log2_enrichment` = +0.828, k=1558/27732) — also a cross-group concordance cell (mean `log2_enrichment` +0.98 across the 6 groups). Under `iid-violation-binomial` this row's p magnitude is co-amplified; this is **not** a magnitude claim.
- `p_floor`: n/a — no exact-test floor for the binomial at these n.
- Cross-group sign partition of the 183 (codon, site) cells: 50 concordant-enriched, 45 concordant-depleted, 88 discordant (the three cross-group sub-tables above; each displays the cells significant in >= 2 of 6 groups — 44, 37, and 25 respectively)
- Rows with `p_adj` < 1e-10: 110 of 1098 (10.0%); each carries `iid-amp` in its flag column
- Per (group, site) BH families (each is 61 codon tests); each cell shows hits at FDR<0.05 (of 61), the smallest p_adj in that family, and the codon that achieves it:

| group | A site | P site | E site |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| BWM_day_0 | 27/61; 6.14e-16 <br>`\newline`{=latex} (TAC) | 28/61; 7.95e-22 <br>`\newline`{=latex} (GCC) | 16/61; 8.65e-42 <br>`\newline`{=latex} (AAG) |
| BWM_day_5 | 27/61; 4.86e-37 <br>`\newline`{=latex} (GAT) | 30/61; 1.75e-48 <br>`\newline`{=latex} (GAT) | 23/61; 1.72e-34 <br>`\newline`{=latex} (AAG) |
| BWM_day_10 | 28/61; 6.67e-21 <br>`\newline`{=latex} (GCC) | 26/61; 5.70e-39 <br>`\newline`{=latex} (GAT) | 16/61; 1.89e-22 <br>`\newline`{=latex} (AAG) |
| control_day_0 | 42/61; 6.48e-60 <br>`\newline`{=latex} (GCT) | 38/61; 3.53e-97 <br>`\newline`{=latex} (GAT) | 30/61; 1.50e-79 <br>`\newline`{=latex} (AAG) |
| control_day_5 | 31/61; 1.33e-24 <br>`\newline`{=latex} (GCC) | 28/61; 4.22e-50 <br>`\newline`{=latex} (GAT) | 26/61; 2.06e-75 <br>`\newline`{=latex} (AAG) |
| control_day_10 | 25/61; 2.45e-15 <br>`\newline`{=latex} (CTC) | 31/61; 1.07e-52 <br>`\newline`{=latex} (GAT) | 19/61; 9.17e-32 <br>`\newline`{=latex} (AAG) |

: Per (group, site) BH family of 61 codons: hits at FDR<0.05, min p_adj, and the codon achieving it. {tbl-colwidths="[22,26,26,26]"}

## Biological interpretation

**The dominant signal of this file is cross-group concordance at codon resolution, not perturbation (concrete).** Of the 183 (codon, site) cells, 50 are concordantly enriched and 45 concordantly depleted across all 6 groups, leaving 88 with at least one cross-group sign disagreement (the partition feeding the three Top-hits tables, each filtered to cells significant in >= 2 of the 6 groups); the largest-magnitude cells do not flip direction across the 6 groups, and the discordant cells that do flip sit at low magnitude or rest on rare-codon counts. The file therefore reads as a structural map of which codons are over- and under-represented at each ribosome site relative to their transcriptome-window frequency, stable across BWM perturbation and the d0/d5/d10 time axis. As in the sister amino-acid CSV, perturbation contrasts live in the per-timepoint Fisher and within-condition Fisher files — this file's biology is the baseline codon-usage shape at stall sites.

**Small and hydroxyl-bearing residues are concordantly depleted across all three sites, each through multiple synonyms (concrete).** Alanine is the clearest case: GCC at all three sites (P -1.02, E -0.94, A -0.90 mean) and GCT at all three sites (E -0.86, A -0.82, P -0.61) are six of the 45 concordantly depleted cells, each with `stall_count` > 100 in every group. The wider depletion table shows alanine is one member of a coherent small/aliphatic cluster: serine (TCC and TCT depleted at all three sites), threonine (ACC at all three sites, plus ACT at A), leucine (CTC at all three sites, plus CTT at A), and valine (GTC at A and P) together make up 22 of the 37 concordantly depleted cells. The amino-acid sister file shows alanine uniformly depleted at all three sites (means -0.74 to -0.82); codon resolution adds that four of these residues are depleted through more than one synonym (Ala GCC+GCT, Ser TCC+TCT, Thr ACC+ACT, Leu CTC+CTT), with the rarer alternatives (e.g. GCA/GCG for Ala) not reaching the concordance set at comparable magnitude. That synonym-spanning is the key codon-level read: the depletion is not a tRNA-isoacceptor effect, which would single out one synonym per residue, but tracks the amino acids themselves — small, often hydroxyl-bearing or short-aliphatic side chains that are mechanistically pause-resistant, the inverse of the charged-residue (K/D/E) enrichment story by the same amino-acid-property logic.

**E:AAG and E:AAA together carry the Lys-at-E concordance signal, with AAG over-represented in cross-group magnitude (concrete; codon-resolution refinement of the AA-sister headline cell).** E:AAA mean `log2_enrichment` +0.78, E:AAG mean +0.60 — both concordant across the 6 groups with `stall_count` >= 105 in every group. AAG carries the `bg-tight` flag (codon `bg_freq` ~0.05) because the tight binomial null at high n returns extreme p magnitudes (E:AAG at control_day_0 is the second-smallest `p_adj` in the file at 1.50e-79). The amino-acid sister CSV's headline E:K cell (mean log2 +0.63) sits between these two codon-level magnitudes, consistent with the AA-level reading being a weighted average over the two Lys codons rather than a single-codon story. Mechanistically, E-site lysine is a textbook ribosome-pause anchor (positive side-chain interacting electrostatically with the negative exit tunnel after translocation); the codon-resolution refinement is that AAA and AAG both contribute, with AAA reaching higher per-cell magnitude than AAG despite AAG being the more common Lys codon.

**P:GAT (Asp) is the codon-level headline cell (concrete; file-min `p_adj`).** P:GAT enriched in all 6 groups at mean `log2_enrichment` +0.98 (range +0.83 to +1.13, `stall_count` 272-1558); the file-minimum `p_adj` is at control_day_0 P:GAT (3.53e-97). The amino-acid sister CSV's P:D cell (mean log2 +0.64) is the strongest enriched concordance cell at AA resolution — codon resolution reveals that the AA-level signal is driven primarily by GAT, with GAC (the other Asp synonym) not appearing in the concordance set at comparable magnitude. This is the cleanest single-synonym story in the file: an AA-level signal that resolves at codon level onto one specific synonym. Mechanistically, P-site aspartate is consistent with peptide-bond chemistry that pauses on acidic residues; the GAC-vs-GAT codon split would point toward tRNA-Asp isoacceptor biology if the underlying signal really is codon-specific rather than aa-property-driven.

**Rare-codon cells reach `|log2_enrichment|` >= 1.0 but are flagged for ranking discipline (exploratory).** The most extreme rare-codon magnitudes are A:ATA (Ile, mean +1.28, `min count` 7) and A:TTA (Leu, up to +2.06 in BWM_day_0, `min count` 5): both are concordant-enriched (positive in all 6 groups) and clear the `#sig` >= 2 floor (A:ATA significant in all 6 groups, A:TTA in 2), so both appear in the A-site block of the concordant-enrichment table carrying `rare-codon (all)` — and because that table ranks by `#sig` before `min count`, A:ATA sorts high in the block on its 6-of-6 significance while A:TTA falls to the last A-site row on its 2-of-6. Their high `|log2_enrichment|` is amplified by the tiny denominator; if the underlying count were off by even a few stall events, the magnitude would swing by ~0.3 log2 or more, which is exactly why the `rare-codon` flag and the `min count` column are read alongside the effect rather than the effect alone. By contrast the higher-count rare-codon cells in the displayed tables (A:TTG, P:GGT, P:AAA on the enriched side; A:TCC, P:CGC on the depleted side) sit at `min count` >= 80 and are reproducible in direction across all 6 groups even where one group's count is low — direction is the usable signal for any rare-codon row, magnitude is not. TGG (Trp, single-codon) cannot be rescued by synonym aggregation in any downstream analysis — the `rare-codon` flag on TGG persists at codon resolution and the amino-acid sister CSV's P:W rare-aa flag is fundamental at this position.

**The headline-grade p magnitudes (down to 1e-97) are iid-violation amplification, not biological strength (concrete).** 110 of 1098 rows (10.0%) carry `p_adj` < 1e-10, including the file-minimum at control_day_0 P:GAT (3.53e-97). The `iid-violation-binomial` caveat names the structural reason: the binomial null assumes 27732 iid Bernoulli draws (one per stall-site position) with p=`bg_freq`, but stall events on the same transcript are not independent (one stall site biases neighbouring positions; per-transcript coverage varies). Combined with common-codon `bg_freq` tightness (Lys-AAG `bg_freq` ~= 0.05, so the binomial null is sharp), modest enrichments at very large n compress to vanishing p-values. Read this file by `log2_enrichment` magnitude and cross-group reproducibility, in that order; treat any `p_adj` < 1e-10 as flagged by `iid-amp` (and `bg-tight` for high-`bg_freq` cells) regardless of how impressive the magnitude looks.

**What to carry forward.** *Concrete*: the cross-group concordance set (50 concordantly enriched + 45 concordantly depleted of the 183 (codon, site) cells) as a structural map of stall-site codon composition that is stable across perturbation and time; P:GAT and E:AAG/AAA as the strongest enriched cells; GCC/GCT (Ala synonyms) depleted uniformly at all three sites as the structural-baseline counterpart; the codon-level refinement of the AA-sister Asp-at-P signal onto GAT specifically (single-synonym), and the Lys-at-E signal onto both AAA and AAG (cross-synonym). *Exploratory*: rare-codon cells (A:ATA, A:TTA at `|log2|` > 1; TGG single-codon throughout) — direction reliable, magnitude not, and no synonym-resolution rescue available for TGG. *Noise*: any per-cell p-magnitude reading above the iid-amplification floor — quote magnitude + reproducibility, not p; the 88 discordant cells are low-magnitude or rare-codon and should not be elevated over the concordant set. Cross-checks worth running in sister files: does the per-timepoint Fisher show BWM-vs-control divergence on top of any of these concordance cells (perturbation overlay on the structural codon-usage map); do P:GAT and E:AAG appear with the same direction in the within-condition Fisher d-vs-d contrasts (perturbation kinetics on top of the stable baseline); does the AA-sister concordance set (E:K, P:D, A:A, etc.) align cleanly with these codon-level cells when re-aggregated.

## Methods

The test is a one-sample binomial (`stall_count` ~ Binomial(`total_n`, `bg_freq`), two-sided), with BH-FDR computed within each (group, site) family of 61 codon tests (each of the 18 sub-families is corrected independently, not against the full 1098). Effect column is `log2_enrichment` = log2(observed `stall_freq` / `bg_freq`); a count-weighted variant `weighted_log2_enrichment` is also present in the CSV but is not used for ranking here (the unweighted log2 is what the binomial p reflects). The test answers "is codon X observed at stall sites at a different frequency than its `bg_freq` in the same group's transcriptome window?", **not** "is codon X's enrichment different between BWM and control" (that is the per-timepoint Fisher) and **not** "is the enrichment shifting across timepoints" (that is the between-timepoint Wilcoxon). Counts (`stall_count`) and totals (`total_n`) are summed across replicates before the test, hence the family-level pseudoreplication caveat. Top hits are presented as three cross-group tables that partition the 183 (codon, site) cells by the sign of `log2_enrichment` across the 6 groups: concordant enrichment (positive in all 6 groups; 50 cells), concordant depletion (negative in all 6; 45 cells), and discordant (at least one sign disagreement; 88 cells). A cell enters the partition only if it has a value in all 6 groups. Each table shows every cell that reached `p_adj` < 0.05 in at least 2 of the 6 groups (`#sig` >= 2, a reproducibility floor on the `#sig` axis rather than a fixed row cap), the kept cells ranked by `#sig` descending then `min count` (the smallest `observed_count` across the 6 groups) descending, then displayed in A / P / E site order; the three tables show 44, 37, and 25 cells respectively. This cross-group view is the lead Top-hits tabulation because the file's dominant structure is reproducibility across groups rather than per-group novelty, and ranking by p magnitude is deliberately avoided since `iid-violation-binomial` and common-codon `bg_freq` tightness inflate p for modest effects at large n.

## What the caveats mean for reading this file

- **pseudorep.** The binomial test pools replicates into one `stall_count` and one `total_n` per (group, site, codon) cell; per-replicate variation is invisible to the statistic, so p-values are anti-conservative — treat the smallest p-magnitudes as inflated, not as additional certainty.
- **iid-violation-binomial.** The binomial null assumes the `total_n` counted positions are i.i.d. (independent and identically distributed — see Family-level caveats), but stall events on the same transcript are correlated and transcripts vary in coverage; this is the dominant reason p-values compress to 1e-22 and beyond (110 rows in the file at `p_adj` < 1e-10). Rank by `log2_enrichment` + `stall_count` + cross-group reproducibility, not by p magnitude.
- **bg-pseudocount-1e-6.** The upstream pipeline floors `bg_freq` at 1e-6 to avoid log2 division-by-zero; does not affect any Top-hits row (all have `bg_freq` > 0.001). Only relevant for genuinely absent codons (e.g. rare AGG, CGG cells), which produce extreme `log2_enrichment` when k>0.
- **bh-per-(group, site).** BH-FDR is per (group, site) family of 61; site-level FDR control is per-61-codon, not pooled across the 183 codons per group. Two rows with the same raw p in different (group, site) families can land at different `p_adj`.
- **larger-bh-family.** Each per-(group, site) BH family is 61 codons, ~3x larger than the AA sister file's 20-amino-acid family. AA-level signals can distribute across synonyms and fall below the per-(group, site) FDR threshold here; if a clear AA-level hit in the sister CSV does not have a matching codon-level row here, the most likely reading is synonym-splitting rather than absence-of-signal.
- **rare-codon-low-count-instability.** Rare codons (ATA, TTA, AGG, GGG, CGG, ACG, TCG, and similar) have unstable `log2_enrichment` estimates because binomial variance at small k is dominated by counting noise. The cross-group tables carry a `rare-codon` flag on any cell where at least one of the 6 groups has `observed_count` < 100, and within each `#sig` tier they sort by `min count` descending so the weakest-supported cells fall to the bottom of their tier: A:ATA and A:TTA are concordant-enriched and clear the `#sig` >= 2 floor (`min count` 7 and 5 across the 6 groups), with A:ATA sorting high in the A-site block on its 6-of-6 significance and A:TTA falling to the last A-site row on its 2-of-6 — exactly the instability this caveat warns about, read by direction and the `min count` column, not by the large magnitude. Read every `rare-codon`-flagged row by direction, not magnitude.
- **p-magnitude-anchored-ranking.** `p_adj` down to 3.53e-97 is best read as a joint readout of (`log2_enrichment` magnitude) x (`total_n`) x (`iid-violation-binomial` amplification factor), not as biological strength. The Top hits flag column applies `iid-amp` to every `p_adj` < 1e-10 row regardless of direction (no selective dampening), and adds `bg-tight` for rows with `bg_freq` > 0.05. Reading priority: `log2_enrichment` magnitude + `stall_count` + cross-group reproducibility, in that order.

### Considered and ruled out

*(none — no caveats were considered-but-not-applicable for this file.)*

---

## Plots — individual

Each panel below is one (group, site) BH-FDR family of 61 codons, plotted as a volcano: `log2_enrichment` (x-range ~+/-10) on the x-axis, -log10(FDR) on the y-axis. Triangles mark FDR<0.05; circles are non-significant. Codons are labelled by three-letter code and coloured by the amino-acid property of their translation (red = acidic, blue = basic, green = hydrophobic, orange = neutral, purple = polar). Horizontal dashed line marks FDR = 0.05; vertical dashed lines mark log2 = +/-0.5. Codons with extreme `|log2_enrichment|` (values up to ~+/-10) mostly sit below the FDR threshold; the labelled high-y points are the high-count common codons at modest `|log2_enrichment|`.

![BWM Day 0 A-site codon unweighted volcano. AAC (Asn, polar) at log2 ~= -1.06, -log10(FDR) ~= 15 is the largest depleted hit. TAC (Tyr, hydrophobic) at log2 ~= +0.82, -log10(FDR) ~= 15 is the largest enriched hit. ACC (Thr, polar), GCT (Ala, hydrophobic), GCC (Ala, hydrophobic) are next-largest depleted hits; TGG (Trp, hydrophobic, rare-codon) is a smaller enriched hit at log2 ~= +0.77.](../../plots/within_condition/codon/individual/unweighted/BWM_day_0_A_volcano.png)

![BWM Day 0 P-site codon unweighted volcano. GCC (Ala, hydrophobic) at log2 ~= -1.16, -log10(FDR) ~= 22 is the largest depleted hit (panel-max y). TCC (Ser, hydrophobic, rare-codon) at log2 ~= -1.28, -log10(FDR) ~= 14 is the largest-magnitude depleted hit. GAT (Asp, acidic) at log2 ~= +0.88, -log10(FDR) ~= 19 is the largest enriched hit. CTC (Leu, hydrophobic), GTC (Val, hydrophobic), CAG (Gln, polar, rare-codon) round out depleted; AAT (Asn, polar, rare-codon), GGT (Gly, neutral, rare-codon) are smaller enriched hits.](../../plots/within_condition/codon/individual/unweighted/BWM_day_0_P_volcano.png)

![BWM Day 0 E-site codon unweighted volcano. AAG (Lys, basic) at log2 ~= +0.69, -log10(FDR) ~= 42 is the panel-max enriched hit. GCC (Ala, hydrophobic) at log2 ~= -0.92, -log10(FDR) ~= 15 and GCT (Ala, hydrophobic) at log2 ~= -0.94, -log10(FDR) ~= 14 are the largest depleted hits. TCC (Ser, hydrophobic, rare-codon), AAA (Lys, basic), AGA (Arg, basic), AAC (Asn, polar) also clear FDR<0.05.](../../plots/within_condition/codon/individual/unweighted/BWM_day_0_E_volcano.png)

![BWM Day 5 A-site codon unweighted volcano. GCC (Ala, hydrophobic) at log2 ~= -1.05, -log10(FDR) ~= 32 is the largest depleted hit. GAT (Asp, acidic) at log2 ~= +0.86, -log10(FDR) ~= 37 is the largest enriched hit (panel-max y). GCT (Ala, hydrophobic), ACC (Thr, polar), TCC (Ser, hydrophobic), CTC (Leu, hydrophobic) round out depleted; TAT (Tyr, hydrophobic), TTG (Leu, hydrophobic), CAT (His, basic), TTT (Phe, hydrophobic, rare-codon) round out enriched.](../../plots/within_condition/codon/individual/unweighted/BWM_day_5_A_volcano.png)

![BWM Day 5 P-site codon unweighted volcano. GAT (Asp, acidic) at log2 ~= +0.96, -log10(FDR) ~= 48 sits as the panel-max enriched hit. GCC (Ala, hydrophobic) at log2 ~= -1.10, -log10(FDR) ~= 35 is the largest depleted hit. TCC (Ser, rare-codon), AAT (Asn, polar), TTT (Phe, rare-codon), TAT (Tyr, hydrophobic), CAT (His) round out enriched; ACC (Thr), CTC (Leu), CAG (Gln) round out depleted.](../../plots/within_condition/codon/individual/unweighted/BWM_day_5_P_volcano.png)

![BWM Day 5 E-site codon unweighted volcano. AAG (Lys, basic) at log2 ~= +0.52, -log10(FDR) ~= 34 sits as the panel-max enriched hit. GCC (Ala, hydrophobic) at log2 ~= -0.94, -log10(FDR) ~= 27 is the largest depleted hit. AAA (Lys, basic), AAT (Asn, polar), GAA (Glu, acidic), AAC (Asn, polar) enrich; GCT (Ala), ACC (Thr), TGC (Cys, hydrophobic, rare-codon), CGC (Arg, basic) deplete.](../../plots/within_condition/codon/individual/unweighted/BWM_day_5_E_volcano.png)

![BWM Day 10 A-site codon unweighted volcano. GCC (Ala, hydrophobic) at log2 ~= -1.09, -log10(FDR) ~= 21 is the largest depleted hit. GAT (Asp, acidic) at log2 ~= +0.78, -log10(FDR) ~= 16 is the largest enriched hit. GAG (Glu, acidic), GGA (Gly, neutral, bg-tight), ATT (Ile, hydrophobic), CAT (His, rare-codon) round out enriched; GCT (Ala), CTC (Leu), ACC (Thr) round out depleted.](../../plots/within_condition/codon/individual/unweighted/BWM_day_10_A_volcano.png)

![BWM Day 10 P-site codon unweighted volcano. GAT (Asp, acidic) at log2 ~= +1.13, -log10(FDR) ~= 39 sits as the panel-max enriched hit. GCC (Ala, hydrophobic) at log2 ~= -0.92, -log10(FDR) ~= 16 is the largest depleted hit. GGT (Gly, rare-codon), AAT (Asn, polar), AAA (Lys, basic) enrich; TCC (Ser, rare-codon), GCT (Ala), CTC (Leu), ACC (Thr) deplete.](../../plots/within_condition/codon/individual/unweighted/BWM_day_10_P_volcano.png)

![BWM Day 10 E-site codon unweighted volcano. AAG (Lys, basic) at log2 ~= +0.52, -log10(FDR) ~= 22 is the largest enriched hit. GCC (Ala, hydrophobic) at log2 ~= -0.89, -log10(FDR) ~= 15 is the largest depleted hit. AAA (Lys, basic), AAT (Asn, polar, rare-codon), GAG (Glu, acidic), AAC (Asn, polar) round out enriched; GCT (Ala), ACC (Thr), TCC (Ser), TAC (Tyr, hydrophobic) round out depleted. Sparsest E-site BWM panel (16 FDR<0.05 codons out of 61).](../../plots/within_condition/codon/individual/unweighted/BWM_day_10_E_volcano.png)

![control Day 0 A-site codon unweighted volcano. GCT (Ala, hydrophobic) at log2 ~= -0.88, -log10(FDR) ~= 60 is the largest-y depleted hit. TAT (Tyr, hydrophobic) at log2 ~= +0.93, -log10(FDR) ~= 32 is one of the largest enriched hits. TAC (Tyr, hydrophobic), TTG (Leu), TTA (Leu, rare-codon, +1.09), ATA (Ile, rare-codon, +1.22) round out enriched; GCC (Ala), AAC (Asn), AGC (Ser, polar), ACC (Thr) deplete. Densest A-site panel in the file (42 FDR<0.05 codons).](../../plots/within_condition/codon/individual/unweighted/control_day_0_A_volcano.png)

![control Day 0 P-site codon unweighted volcano. GAT (Asp, acidic) at log2 ~= +0.83, -log10(FDR) ~= 97 sits at the panel maximum y — the file-min `p_adj` cell. GCC (Ala, hydrophobic) at log2 ~= -1.06, -log10(FDR) ~= 72 is the largest depleted hit. TAT (Tyr), AAT (Asn, polar), CAT (His, basic), AAA (Lys, basic) enrich; TCC (Ser, rare-codon), CTC (Leu), GCT (Ala), CGC (Arg, rare-codon) deplete.](../../plots/within_condition/codon/individual/unweighted/control_day_0_P_volcano.png)

![control Day 0 E-site codon unweighted volcano. AAG (Lys, basic) at log2 ~= +0.57, -log10(FDR) ~= 80 sits at the panel maximum y. GCT (Ala, hydrophobic) at log2 ~= -0.94, -log10(FDR) ~= 67 and GCC (Ala) at log2 ~= -0.90, -log10(FDR) ~= 56 are the largest depleted hits. AAA (Lys), AGA (Arg, basic), AAC (Asn, polar), AAT (Asn, polar) enrich; TGC (Cys, rare-codon), TGT (Cys, rare-codon), CGC (Arg, rare-codon) deplete.](../../plots/within_condition/codon/individual/unweighted/control_day_0_E_volcano.png)

![control Day 5 A-site codon unweighted volcano. GAT (Asp, acidic) at log2 ~= +0.71, -log10(FDR) ~= 22 is one of the larger enriched hits; GCC (Ala, hydrophobic) at log2 ~= -0.95, -log10(FDR) ~= 25 is the largest depleted hit. TAT (Tyr, rare-codon, +0.76), CAT (His), AAT (Asn, polar), GAA (Glu, acidic) round out enriched; CTC (Leu), GCT (Ala), ACC (Thr), TCC (Ser) round out depleted.](../../plots/within_condition/codon/individual/unweighted/control_day_5_A_volcano.png)

![control Day 5 P-site codon unweighted volcano. GAT (Asp, acidic) at log2 ~= +1.00, -log10(FDR) ~= 50 sits at the panel maximum y. GCC (Ala, hydrophobic) at log2 ~= -0.85, -log10(FDR) ~= 21 is the largest depleted hit. TTT (Phe, rare-codon, +0.78), GGT (Gly, +0.73), AAT (Asn, polar), TAT (Tyr, rare-codon, +0.62) enrich; TCC (Ser), CAG (Gln, rare-codon), TGG (Trp, rare-codon), CTC (Leu) deplete.](../../plots/within_condition/codon/individual/unweighted/control_day_5_P_volcano.png)

![control Day 5 E-site codon unweighted volcano. AAG (Lys, basic) at log2 ~= +0.75, -log10(FDR) ~= 75 sits at the panel maximum y. GCC (Ala, hydrophobic) at log2 ~= -1.19, -log10(FDR) ~= 35 and GCT (Ala) at log2 ~= -0.98, -log10(FDR) ~= 27 are the largest depleted hits. AAA (Lys), GAG (Glu, acidic), AAT (Asn, polar), AGA (Arg, basic) enrich; TGC (Cys, rare-codon), ACC (Thr), TAC (Tyr) deplete.](../../plots/within_condition/codon/individual/unweighted/control_day_5_E_volcano.png)

![control Day 10 A-site codon unweighted volcano. GAT (Asp, acidic) at log2 ~= +0.67, -log10(FDR) ~= 15 is the largest enriched hit. CTC (Leu, hydrophobic) at log2 ~= -0.81, -log10(FDR) ~= 15 and GCT (Ala) at log2 ~= -0.77, -log10(FDR) ~= 15 are the largest depleted hits. TAT (Tyr, rare-codon, +0.89), TTG (Leu), TAC (Tyr), ATT (Ile) enrich; GCC (Ala), AAC (Asn), CAC (His, rare-codon) deplete.](../../plots/within_condition/codon/individual/unweighted/control_day_10_A_volcano.png)

![control Day 10 P-site codon unweighted volcano. GAT (Asp, acidic) at log2 ~= +1.11, -log10(FDR) ~= 53 sits at the panel maximum y. GCC (Ala, hydrophobic) at log2 ~= -1.00, -log10(FDR) ~= 25 is the largest depleted hit. TAT (Tyr, rare-codon, +1.12), AAT (Asn, polar), GGT (Gly), CAT (His, rare-codon) enrich; TCC (Ser, rare-codon), CTC (Leu), GTC (Val), CAG (Gln, rare-codon) deplete. Densest P-site panel for control_day_10 (31 FDR<0.05 codons).](../../plots/within_condition/codon/individual/unweighted/control_day_10_P_volcano.png)

![control Day 10 E-site codon unweighted volcano. AAG (Lys, basic) at log2 ~= +0.55, -log10(FDR) ~= 32 sits at the panel maximum y. GCC (Ala, hydrophobic) at log2 ~= -0.80, -log10(FDR) ~= 17 and GCT (Ala) at log2 ~= -0.79, -log10(FDR) ~= 16 are the largest depleted hits. AAA (Lys), AAT (Asn, polar), AAC (Asn, polar), GAA (Glu, acidic) enrich; CGT (Arg), CGC (Arg, rare-codon), TCT (Ser, rare-codon) deplete.](../../plots/within_condition/codon/individual/unweighted/control_day_10_E_volcano.png)
